_comment: captions.json contract for publish.py (F1). One UNIQUE caption per platform (SOUL §6 — never verbatim-shared). A value is a STRING (single post) or an OBJECT {text, thread[], title}. Keyed by brief platform-name (x, tiktok, instagram, threads, facebook, youtube). X must be ≤280 chars/post and 0 hashtags. Labs posts must carry the RUO line somewhere in the post (incl. thread). This file mirrors the real ACME-011 BPC-157 carousel that published to X (thread) + TikTok.

x.text: Everyone talks about BPC-157 like the science is settled. It isn't — and that's exactly what makes it worth a closer look. A short thread on what the research actually shows.
thread: In preclinical models, BPC-157 upregulates VEGFR2 — a receptor that drives blood-vessel formation — and engages VEGF and ERK1/2 signaling tied to tissue perfusion. The mechanism is genuinely interesting.
Animal studies also report increased collagen deposition and fibroblast migration across tendon, muscle, bone, skin and gut — plus modulation of nitric-oxide signaling.

The honest part: human evidence is limited to a few tiny pilot studies. No FDA approval, WADA-prohibited (2025). Promising ≠ proven. (Yuan et al., Int. J. Mol. Sci., 2026)

For research use only — not for human consumption.

tiktok:
BPC-157 keeps going viral — but what does the research actually say?

The mechanism is real in preclinical models: blood-vessel signaling (VEGFR2), collagen and fibroblast activity, and tissue-repair pathways studied across tendon, muscle, bone, skin and gut.

The honest part: human data is still limited to a few small pilot studies. Promising, not proven.

Source: Yuan et al., Int. J. Mol. Sci. 2026.
For research use only — not for human consumption.

youtube.text: BPC-157 is one of the most-discussed research peptides. Here's the honest preclinical picture — mechanism vs. the limits of current human data. For research use only — not for human consumption.
